name: Priene Athena
description: The Temple of Athena Polias in Priene was a significant sanctuary dedicated to the Greek goddess Athena, the patron deity of the city. Located in the ancient city of Priene, in present-day Turkey, the temple was a prominent feature of the city's religious and civic life. Designed by Pytheos, the architect of the Mausoleum at Halicarnassus, it exemplified the Ionic order and was considered a masterpiece of Hellenistic architecture. The temple housed a cult statue of Athena Polias, the protector of the city, and served as a focal point for religious ceremonies, festivals, and dedications. Although not directly involved in major mythological narratives, the temple itself represented the enduring devotion to Athena and the importance of her role in safeguarding the city and its inhabitants. The temple's construction and maintenance were crucial for maintaining Athena's favor and ensuring the prosperity and well-being of Priene. Its ruins stand as a testament to the rich cultural and religious heritage of ancient Greece and the enduring influence of Athena's cult.
subtitle: Temple of Athena Polias in Priene

keyMyths.title: Foundation of the Temple
keyMyths.description: The temple's construction signified Priene's dedication to Athena, seeking her protection and blessings. It was a physical manifestation of their devotion and a plea for continued prosperity.

keyMyths.title: Rituals and Festivals
keyMyths.description: The temple was the site of various religious rituals and festivals honoring Athena. These events reinforced the community's connection to the goddess and maintained her favor.

sources.author: Vitruvius
sources.description: Discusses the principles of architecture and mentions Pytheos, the architect of the Temple of Athena.
sources.text: Discusses the principles of architecture and mentions Pytheos, the architect of the Temple of Athena.

sources.author: J.J. Pollitt
sources.work: Art and Experience in Classical Greece
sources.description: Provides insights into the art and architecture of ancient Greece, including temples like the one in Priene.
